analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition
analysis.dtype: float32 (inferred: nn.Linear default + torch.randn default, no autocast in workload)

bottleneck.description: Every Linear/GEMM in the model runs on the cuBLAS FP32 SIMT (CUDA-core) path with the Tensor Core pipeline completely idle. The hardware counter smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active.avg.pct_of_peak_sustained_active reads 0.0 on all GEMM kernels, and tensor_core_active_pct is 0.0 in every aggregate. The model is float32 (nn.Linear default weights + torch.randn float32 input, no autocast), so cuBLAS dispatches the FP32 SIMT kernel ('Kernel2' is a demangle-failed cuBLAS GEMM running at 200-210 registers/thread) instead of the WGMMA BF16 Tensor Core path. On this Blackwell part peak BF16 is 1457 TFLOPS, so the FP32 SIMT path leaves the dominant matrix engine unused. The kernels are neither compute-saturated nor memory-bound: sm__throughput is 6-23%, dram__throughput is 3-9%, achieved_occupancy is 8-17%, and eligible_cycles_pct is 12-36% (several GEMMs <20, the Blackwell latency-bound indicator since warp_cycles_per_instruction is removed on Blackwell). Symptomatic of this misrouting: the [2048->512] GEMM (op_id=9/19, 137440 ns) is SLOWER than the 4x-larger [2048->2048] GEMM (op_id=5, 125536 ns) because the FP32 SIMT kernel tiles the N=512 output poorly (grid (8,1,22)=176 blocks, occupancy 8.3%, sm_throughput 6.2%).

evidence.kernel: Kernel2 (cuBLAS FP32 SIMT GEMM; demangled_name null)

transformation.description: Cast the model parameters and the input activation to torch.bfloat16 so cuBLAS routes every aten.mm.default / aten.addmm.default to the Blackwell WGMMA BF16 Tensor Core path. BF16 satisfies the 64/64/16 WGMMA tile minimum for these dims (M=256, N/K in {512,2048}), engaging the matrix engine that is currently 0% utilized. This is the highest-leverage change because all 8 GEMM kernels (and the two aggregate buckets that re-count them) share the identical root cause. Per fx-patterns.md taxonomy, dtype is a non-graph change applied in get_model_and_input(), NOT inside the backend, because the dtype of placeholder tensors is not editable from within the Aten IR graph. The Aten IR nodes affected are aten.mm.default and aten.addmm.default; verify post-change that 'Kernel2' is replaced by an sm100/sm90 xmma bf16 WGMMA kernel and tensor_core_active_pct > 0.
fx_steps: # Non-graph change in get_model_and_input() (NOT an FX pass):
model = MLPActivations().to(DEVICE).eval().to(torch.bfloat16)
x = torch.randn(BATCH_SIZE, DIM_IN, device=DEVICE, dtype=torch.bfloat16)
return model, x
# Equivalent alternative without editing dtype of params: wrap forward in
#   with torch.autocast('cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16): ...
# but explicit .bfloat16() is preferred so cuBLAS sees BF16 weights directly.
# Verification: after recompile, assert no kernel reports tensor_core_active_pct == 0
transformation.code_hint: model.to(torch.bfloat16); x = x.to(torch.bfloat16)  # routes aten.mm/aten.addmm to WGMMA BF16 Tensor Cores

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: ~50% on GEMM operand reads (FP32 4 bytes -> BF16 2 bytes for weights and activations); the kernels are not memory-bound today so the principal win is Tensor Core engagement, not bandwidth

optimizations.notes: Impact is expressed as a within-profile relative figure only: duration_ns is 2-5x inflated by ncu counter collection and the aten::mm_0 / prologue aggregate buckets double-count kernels already attributed to the per-shape aten::mm ops (see fused_kernel_double_count flag). The true non-overlapping GEMM cost is the 8 per-shape kernels = 875326 ns. FP32-SIMT-bound GEMMs typically see 3-8x on the Blackwell BF16 WGMMA path; the 60% figure is deliberately conservative and accounts for the small M=256 batch limiting peak utilization. Validate numerical tolerance (BF16 has ~3 decimal digits) against the FP32 baseline output.

bottleneck.description: Inductor currently lowers each Linear as a separate cuBLAS GEMM ('Kernel2') followed by a distinct pointwise Triton kernel (triton_poi_fused_addmm_relu/gelu/silu/tanh) that does the bias-add plus activation. That means each layer writes its GEMM output to DRAM, then relaunches a kernel to read it back, apply bias+activation, and write again. The activation kernels run at only 6-30% dram throughput and 5-22% issue activity (triton_poi_fused_addmm_tanh_3: sm_throughput 1.2%, occupancy 6.7%) - they are launch- and round-trip-dominated, not work-dominated. There are 8 such pointwise launches plus the GEMM launches, all serialized on stream 7.

transformation.description: Recompile with Inductor max-autotune so it emits a Triton template GEMM (triton_tem_fused_*) that performs bias-add and the activation in the GEMM epilogue, fusing each aten.addmm.default + aten.relu/gelu/silu/tanh into a single kernel. This eliminates the separate pointwise launch per layer and removes the DRAM round-trip of the GEMM output (the [256,2048] intermediates are ~1 MB in BF16 and fit comfortably in the large Blackwell L2, so the saved traffic is real). max-autotune also re-tiles the awkward N=512 GEMM (op_id=9/19) that the default cuBLAS heuristic tiled poorly. This is a backend/compile-mode change, not a hand-written FX pass; the Aten IR targets it acts on are aten.addmm.default and the trailing activation nodes aten.relu.default / aten.gelu.default / aten.silu.default / aten.tanh.default.
transformation.location: torch.compile call site / backend (run_workload.py compile step), mode='max-autotune'
fx_steps: # Compile-mode change, not manual graph surgery:
compiled = torch.compile(model, mode='max-autotune')
# Inductor's epilogue-fusion + GEMM autotuner will fuse
#   aten.addmm.default -> aten.relu/gelu/silu/tanh into a triton_tem_fused_* GEMM.
# If driving Inductor directly via compile_fx_inner, set max-autotune config:
#   torch._inductor.config.max_autotune = True
#   torch._inductor.config.max_autotune_gemm = True
transformation.code_hint: torch.compile(model, mode='max-autotune')  # GEMM epilogue fuses bias+activation, eliminates pointwise relaunch

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates one full read+write round-trip of each layer's GEMM output (4 intermediates of ~0.5-1 MB each in BF16) by fusing the activation into the GEMM epilogue

optimizations.notes: Must run AFTER OPT-1: the Triton template GEMM and any tile autotuning are selected for the runtime dtype, and a Tensor Core epilogue fusion is only worthwhile once the GEMM body itself is on the WGMMA path. Standalone impact is small (~0.7% of attributed time lives in the pointwise activations) but the launch-count reduction and the autotuned tiling of the N=512 GEMM compound with OPT-1. Capped at medium confidence: max-autotune kernel selection is workload/driver dependent and not guaranteed to beat cuBLAS for every shape (MCP search tools unavailable; relying on fx-patterns.md and Inductor defaults).

bottleneck.description: Even after BF16 engages Tensor Cores (OPT-1), the four square [256,2048]x[2048,2048] GEMMs (op_id=5/7/15/17) carry the most MACs (256*2048*2048 = 1.07G each) and dominate residual GEMM time. On this Blackwell server part FP8 (e4m3) Tensor Cores deliver substantially higher matrix throughput than BF16, and these K=2048 contractions satisfy the FP8 64/64/32 tile minimum. The GEMMs are currently latency/occupancy-starved (eligible_cycles 36%, occupancy 16.6%) so doubling matrix-engine throughput per issued instruction directly attacks the residual cost.

transformation.description: For the four square hidden GEMMs, evaluate row/tensor-wise FP8 (torch.float8_e4m3fn) via torch._scaled_mm, keeping accumulation in BF16/FP32 and applying per-tensor scales. The Aten IR target is aten.mm.default for these shapes; an FP8 pass would read the BF16 weight buffer (after OPT-1), quantize with a computed scale, register the FP8 weight as a buffer, and replace aten.mm.default with a scaled-mm call. This is the most aggressive option and is accuracy-sensitive.
transformation.location: Aten IR pass inside _aten_inner_compile (after OPT-1 dtype cast), or _scaled_mm at the module level
fx_steps: # Conceptual FX surgery (medium-risk, validate accuracy first):
# 1. placeholders = [n for n in gm.graph.nodes if n.op == 'placeholder']
# 2. ph_to_tensor = {ph: t for ph, t in zip(placeholders, real_inputs)}
# 3. for mm in nodes where mm.target is torch.ops.aten.mm.default and K==2048:
#       W = ph_to_tensor[...]  # BF16 weight after OPT-1
#       scale_w = W.abs().amax() / 448.0   # e4m3 max
#       W_fp8 = (W / scale_w).to(torch.float8_e4m3fn)
#       gm.register_buffer('_fp8_w_{i}', W_fp8)   # buffer alloc AFTER dtype promotion
#       replace mm with torch.ops.aten._scaled_mm(x_fp8, W_fp8, scale_x, scale_w, out_dtype=torch.bfloat16)
# 4. gm.graph.lint(); gm.recompile()

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: ~50% further reduction in weight-read bytes vs BF16 (1 byte vs 2) on the four square GEMMs

optimizations.notes: Must run AFTER OPT-1 (FP8 weight buffers are derived from the BF16 weights and register_buffer must follow dtype promotion). FP8 on this Blackwell part requires torch >= 2.1 and cuBLAS >= 12.4; verify torch.cuda.get_device_capability() >= (9, 0) and that _scaled_mm selects an FP8 Tensor Core kernel. Low confidence: accuracy impact for a 4-layer MLP must be validated against the FP32 baseline, per-tensor scaling may be insufficient for the GELU/SiLU activations' dynamic range, and the small M=256 batch may not amortize FP8 quantize/dequantize overhead. Treat as an experiment gated on OPT-1 results. MCP exa-search was unavailable to corroborate this transform, so it is capped at low per fallback policy.

global_notes: 1 operator below 1% threshold omitted (combined 0.70% of attributed time): aten::addmm_0 (the standalone bias+activation epilogue bucket, 15552 ns). Its kernels are analyzed indirectly under OPT-2.

fused_kernel_double_count: the two largest budget entries (aten::mm_0 = 33.26% and layer::unique::prologue_0 = 26.88%) are aggregate/representative buckets that RE-COUNT the same physical kernels attributed to the individual per-shape aten::mm and aten::addmm operators. The true non-overlapping GEMM cost is the 8 per-shape kernels (875326 ns). All percentages are computed against total_attributed_ns = 2235099 ns per protocol, but the overlap means absolute time figures must not be summed naively.

dynamic_shapes flag: every GEMM kernel shares the demangle-failed name 'Kernel2' yet durations range 50592-137855 ns (CV > 50%). This is a kernel-NAME collision across distinct GEMM shapes (a cuBLAS GEMM whose symbol failed to demangle), NOT genuine shape polymorphism of a single kernel. Shape disambiguation comes from the aten::mm operator_id metadata, not the kernel name.

Blackwell counter note: warp_cycles_per_instruction is null on this architecture as expected (removed on Blackwell). Latency-bound assessment uses eligible_cycles_pct < 20 instead; the [2048->512] GEMMs (op_id=9/19) sit at 12.04% -> latency/occupancy-bound.

Root-cause unification: tensor_core_active_pct == 0.0 on 100% of GEMM kernels is the single dominant finding. OPT-1 (BF16 dtype promotion) addresses every GEMM operator at once; OPT-2 and OPT-3 are strictly downstream of it in the dependency DAG (OPT-1 -> {OPT-2, OPT-3}).

MCP tools (context7, exa-search, memory, sequential-thinking) were not available in this environment; analysis used the bundled knowledge files fx-patterns.md and hardware-limits.md per the documented fallback. Novel/aggressive transforms (OPT-3 FP8) are capped at low confidence accordingly.
No multi_stream_overlap (all kernels stream_id=7), no cuda_graph_replay (compile_mode='inductor'), no high_unattributed (unattributed_kernels is empty), no clock_domain anomaly.
